landmark.name: The Spire
landmark.visualDescription: A towering pre-collapse stone church with a distinctive pointed steeple rising 90 feet above the settlement. The main structure is weathered gray masonry with boarded windows and vines creeping up the north face. The bell tower remains intact, housing a cracked bronze bell used to signal gatherings. Makeshift scaffolding of salvaged metal supports the southwest corner where stonework has crumbled. The main entrance features reinforced wooden doors carved with faded religious symbols.

keyLocations.name: Sister's Infirmary
keyLocations.visualDescription: A converted stone cottage attached to the church's eastern wing. Red crosses are painted above the entrance. Inside, rows of salvaged hospital beds line whitewashed walls. Shelves hold bottled herbs, bandages, and pre-collapse medical supplies. Natural light streams through large repaired windows. Smoke from a small hearth carries the scent of medicinal herbs.

keyLocations.name: The Pilgrim's Rest
keyLocations.visualDescription: A timber-framed hall with a collapsed south wall partially rebuilt with stone blocks and wood planking. Long communal tables dominate the interior, carved with pilgrim names and dates. A makeshift bar runs along the western side, stocked with bottled spirits and well water. A large hearth in the center provides warmth and cooking capability. Worn tapestries depicting religious scenes hang from the rafters.

keyLocations.name: Supply Stores
keyLocations.visualDescription: A sprawling compound of interconnected metal shipping containers and repurposed warehouse buildings with canvas-patched roofs. Reinforced wooden crates are stacked neatly behind chain-link fencing. Multiple locked storage sections are organized by category—food, water, ammunition, and trade goods. Solar panels jury-rigged to the roof provide power for climate control in the food storage area.

keyLocations.name: Sister Catherine's Workshop
keyLocations.visualDescription: A cramped stone outbuilding with a thatched roof held down by rope and wire. The interior is cluttered with workbenches—anvil, grinding wheel, and crafting tools line the walls. Jars of supplies fill floor-to-ceiling shelves: thread, leather scraps, metal offcuts, and tool handles. The single window glows with forge light during working hours. A venting pipe extends through the roof, carrying smoke and sparks.

keyLocations.name: The Confession Booth
keyLocations.visualDescription: A wooden structure inside the church's nave, partitioned into a traditional two-sided confession chamber. Its dark wood is polished from centuries of penitents. A small oil lamp provides dim light. Behind one side sits Sister Eleanor, the settlement's elder, dispensing advice, blessings, and requests for aid. The air carries the scent of incense and age.

keyLocations.name: The Scavenger's Market
keyLocations.visualDescription: An open-air market built in the church courtyard, with merchant stalls sheltered under salvaged corrugated metal roofs. Weathered tables display pre-collapse goods, ammunition, clothing, and curated salvage. Canvas awnings stripe the area in faded colors. Goods hang from rope strung between wooden posts—lanterns, canteens, and tools catch the light. A central fountain (dry) once marked the courtyard center.

keyLocations.name: The Garden Sanctuary
keyLocations.visualDescription: A walled garden on the church's south side, enclosed by rebuilt stone walls with ivy. Rows of cultivated medicinal herbs, vegetables, and flowering plants grow in raised beds made from reclaimed lumber. A small stone shrine sits at the garden's center, adorned with flowers and personal offerings. Wooden benches and a meditation platform overlook the peaceful space. A hand-pumped well provides water for irrigation.

hazards.description: Seasonal water runoff from the surrounding lowlands collects in the southern sections of the settlement, particularly during spring thaw and heavy rains. Muddy pools form in low-lying areas, and the paths become treacherous with overflow from the degraded drainage systems.

The eastern wing of the original church complex shows severe structural decay. Weakened roof timbers and crumbling stone walls create a constant danger of falling debris. Several sections have already partially collapsed, leaving gaps and unstable rubble.
hazards.locationHint: eastern edge of The Spire and attached buildings

hazards.description: Pre-collapse industrial ruins lie buried in a nearby ravine to the north. Residual radiation from these ruins creates a faint but persistent hazard that increases closer to the source. The settlement maintains a buffer zone and warns travelers not to venture beyond marked boundaries.
hazards.locationHint: north perimeter, beyond the settlement walls
